intro: Callista beckleri, also known as the golden bird orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The golden bird orchid is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including wound healing, immune system support, and the alleviation of various respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Callista beckleri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from the golden bird orchid include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often used to treat respiratory problems, skin conditions, and infections.
